source: All-In Podcast — E125: SpaceX Starship launch, Fox News settlement, AI/Reddit
summary: Reddit, Stack Overflow, and other large proprietary data corpus owners will successfully monetize their data for AI training
summary_extended: Baker (and co-hosts Chamath and Jason on the All-In Podcast) argue that large proprietary data corpus owners are entering a new monetization phase, where they will charge AI companies for API and training data access rather than providing it freely. The thesis is grounded in two concrete, near-simultaneous announcements: Reddit's plan to charge for data access and Stack Overflow's similar move, both occurring in the same week in April 2023. The reasoning is that AI model training is highly dependent on high-quality, human-generated text data, giving corpus owners meaningful leverage — analogous to how robots.txt gave websites control over web crawlers, a new 'ai.txt' standard may formalize this dynamic. The investment implication is that Reddit (IPO pending) and similar data-rich platforms could unlock a new, high-margin revenue stream from AI companies like OpenAI and Google, potentially improving their monetization profiles ahead of or following public listings. No specific revenue estimates or valuation figures were provided, but the trend is framed as near-term and accelerating given the pace of AI adoption.
detail: Reddit announced plans to charge for API/data access for AI training. Stack Overflow followed. Chamath and Jason frame this as an emerging trend where data corpus owners (Quora, Yelp, App Store reviews, Amazon reviews, Craigslist) will demand payment or restrict access. The analogy to robots.txt is made — an 'ai.txt' standard may emerge. Sam Altman's investment in Reddit creates a conflict-of-interest dynamic.
confidence_evidence: Framed as an emerging trend with two concrete examples (Reddit, Stack Overflow) announced in the same week. No specific valuation or monetization estimates given.
quote_fragment: Reddit announced plans to start charging companies that use its data to train AI models

ticker_audit.reasoning: GOOGL is mentioned as a potential data buyer, but the thesis is about data corpus owners monetizing their data — GOOGL is a large AI company that would pay for data, not a beneficiary of this trend. Its inclusion as a buyer does not make it a direct subject or beneficiary of the thesis, and it has its own vast proprietary data. Removing as tangential.
